facts.text: Collinsville grew around the Collins Company axe factory, founded in 1826 by Samuel Collins and brothers, producing high‑quality axes that powered the town’s early industry.
The former Collins Company factory now houses the Canton Historical Museum, where a research library upstairs holds photographs of the 1955 flood.
A converted railroad line that once ran through the factory has become the Farmington Valley Greenway Rail Trail, a popular spot for walkers and cyclists.
In 1924 the factory building was turned into a recreational facility for workers, reflecting the company’s investment in employee well‑being.
Collinsville’s main street still echoes its Victorian roots, with ornate early‑20th‑century architecture that earned the area a National Register historic district designation.
The town hosts an annual Halloween Parade that draws locals and visitors alike, showcasing Collinsville’s community spirit.
